data.title: Ouster prices $200M public stock offering at $55.22/share
Verisk estimates Venezuela earthquake losses to exceed $10B
data.description:
• Verisk (VRSK) estimated that economic losses from the June 24 earthquake sequence in Venezuela will likely exceed $10B.
  • Verisk (VRSK) said uncertainty around insured losses is higher than usual because of Venezuela's elevated inflation, low insurance penetration, sanctions-related market complexities, and challenges in valuing insured assets.
  • The earthquakes caused the heaviest damage in the Caracas metropolitan area and La Guaira state, with an estimated 1,400 buildings destroyed. Significant destruction was also reported across Aragua, Carabobo and Yaracuy states.
  • The stock price traded marginally lower on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Hyperscale Data increases bitcoin holdings by 67 to roughly 849
data.description:
• Hyperscale Data (GPUS) has purchased 67 additional Bitcoin between June 30 and July 1, 2026, bringing its total Bitcoin treasury to approximately 849 BTC.
  • "We continue to believe in Bitcoin as a foundational asset of the future digital economy and of the Company's balance sheet," stated Milton "Todd" Ault III, Executive Chairman of Hyperscale Data.
  • Press Release

data.title: Ensign Group buys two nursing facilities in Texas
data.description:
• The Ensign Group (ENSG) said it has acquired the real estate and operations of two skilled nursing facilities in Texas: the 126-bed Las Ventanas de Socorro in Socorro and the 124-bed Los Arcos del Norte Care Center in El Paso.
  • The properties were acquired by subsidiaries of Standard Bearer Healthcare REIT, Ensign's captive real estate company, while the facilities will be operated by Ensign-affiliated tenants.
  • The acquisitions became effective on July 1. Following the deal, Ensign's portfolio has expanded to 398 healthcare operations, including 48 senior living operations, across 17 states.

data.title: Yiren Digital sets $20M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Yiren Digital (YRD) board has authorized a new share repurchase program, under which the company may repurchase up to 10% of total issued and outstanding ordinary shares and/or American depositary shares for up to US$20M over the next 12 months, effective as of the date hereof.
  • The company expects to fund the repurchases from its existing cash balance.
  • YRD shares rose +23% premarket to $1.11.

data.title: Northern Star appoints new CEO as Elliott presses for changes
AM Need to Know: OpenAI stake talks, Google antitrust setback & more
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Thursday as investors awaited the closely watched nonfarm payrolls report, due a day early ahead of the July 4 Independence Day holiday.

Now, here are four news stories that broke overnight to watch out for:

OpenAI (OPENAI) discusses U.S. government stake: The $852 billion artificial intelligence startup has  discussed offering a 5% equity stake to the U.S. government as it seeks to clear political and regulatory obstacles under the Trump administration, the Financial Times reported. CEO Sam Altman has been in active talks with President Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, arguing that giving the public a financial stake is the best way to share AI's upside. The proposed arrangement would involve other U.S. AI companies handing over a similar stake, though it remains unclear whether rivals would be willing to participate. 

Google loses EU antitrust appeal: Alphabet's Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) has  lost its final appeal in European courts, with the European Court of Justice upholding a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) EU antitrust fine for abusing the market dominance of its Android operating system. The ruling is legally binding and marks a major victory for the Brussels-based regulator in a case that began when the penalty was imposed in 2018. 

Nvidia partners on AI factories: Chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has  introduced a revenue-sharing and credit-support model with AI cloud companies to deploy large-scale, multi-tenant AI factories. Sharon AI and Firmus are among the first companies to work with Nvidia on this strategy, with Sharon AI deploying up to 40,000 Grace Blackwell GB300 GPUs. The arrangement accelerates adoption of Nvidia platforms while giving cloud providers a capital-efficient path to scale and providing Nvidia with a new recurring, usage-linked earnings stream. 

Apple plans ambitious iPhone launch: Apple  (AAPL) is  preparing an ambitious iPhone launch for the second half of this year and first half of 2027 with at least five new models and a larger volume of foldable handsets than previously expected, Nikkei Asia reported. The company has asked suppliers to prepare to manufacture about 10 million foldable iPhones this year, up from an earlier forecast of 7 million to 8 million units. The push coincides with an accelerating race among smartphone makers to broaden their foldable offerings, a high-growth segment currently led by Samsung Electronics and Chinese competitors like Huawei, as Apple looks to gain market share amid an industrywide component supply crunch.

data.title: Trump Accounts to launch without rollover option: report
data.description:
The Trump administration is expected to roll out Trump Accounts next week without allowing firms to host the children's savings accounts on their own platforms, Semafor reported, citing people familiar with the discussions.

Companies including Chime (CHYM), Empower, Fidelity, and SoFi (SOFI) had hoped to receive approval to roll over the accounts from the Treasury Department's app, developed in partnership with Bank of New York Mellon (BNY) and Robinhood (HOOD)—in time for the program's launch.

Those firms are now expecting guidance by August at the latest, one of the people told Semafor. “We expect [Treasury officials] to define requirements and work with firms to figure out who’s best situated to host rollover accounts,” the person said, adding that the rollover accounts will boost signups because “more people can participate through their trusted provider.”

Trump Accounts provide eligible American children with tax-advantaged investment accounts. Eligible children born in the years 2025 through 2028 qualify for an initial $1,000 contribution from the U.S. Treasury. Family, friends, and employers can collectively contribute up to $5,000 per year.

The contributions are automatically invested in a default qualifying low-cost index fund designed for long-term growth potential. According to experts cited by Semafor, more than half of the children eligible for the program's $1,000 seed deposit have yet to enroll.

SpaceX (SPCX) has reportedly discussed the possibility of contributing shares to the children's savings program. Earlier this week, Micron Technology (MU) said it has invested $250M to increase long-term savings opportunities for children and families through Trump Accounts.

data.title: SoftBank to set up SB Neo to offer AI compute services in US
data.description:
SoftBank Group (SFTBY) (SFTBF) and its telecom unit SoftBank Corp. is establishing a company called SB Neo, Inc. that will operate a neocloud business in the U.S. to provide AI computing resources from next year.

The Japanese conglomerate said SB Neo will be established in the U.S. this month, and 51% will be owned by SoftBank Corp. and 49% will be owned by SoftBank Group Corp., and a consolidated subsidiary of SoftBank Corp.

The company said that using SoftBank Group's 10-gigawatt-scale energy and AI infrastructure currently under development, in the fiscal year ending March 31, 2028 (FY 2027), SB Neo will launch neocloud services that provide major U.S. enterprises, including hyperscalers, with the computing resources needed for large-scale AI model training and inference.

SoftBank added that to meet the strong demand for AI computing resources in the U.S., SB Neo will expand its service capacity in phases, with plans to later deploy AI infrastructure on a 10-GW scale.

The company noted that in Japan, SoftBank Corp. has been providing a beta version of its GPU cloud service powered by 'Infrinia AI Cloud OS,' a software stack for AI data centers, since May.

SoftBank Group is already advancing large-scale AI infrastructure projects in the U.S. and will support SB Neo's business by using its infrastructure, personnel, and other resources, the company added.

SoftBank Corp also plans to proceed with the construction of gigawatt-scale AI data centers in Japan.

"With the strong demand for AI data centers in the United States and the SoftBank Group making steady progress toward securing 10 gigawatts of power, we are partnering with SoftBank Group Corp. to develop our neocloud business in the United States. We also plan to proceed with the construction of gigawatt-scale AI data centers in Japan as soon as preparations are in place," said SoftBank Corp.'s President and CEO Junichi Miyakawa.

On Wednesday it was reported that SoftBank is in discussions with lenders to secure a $10B margin loan that's backed by its stake in OpenAI (OPENAI).
